howItWorks.intro: This vitamin d calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Dose based on gap between current and target levels
Average increase: ~8 ng/mL per month at 5000 IU/day
howItWorks.outro: Health metrics are general guidelines. Consult your healthcare provider for personalized medical advice.

faq.question: How much vitamin D do I need?
faq.answer: The RDA is 600-800 IU, but many experts recommend 2,000-5,000 IU daily to reach optimal levels of 40-60 ng/mL. Those with deficiency may need higher doses temporarily.

faq.question: What is a good vitamin D level?
faq.answer: Under 20 ng/mL is deficient. 20-29 is insufficient. 30-50 is adequate. 40-60 is considered optimal by many functional medicine practitioners. Over 100 is potentially toxic.

faq.question: How common is deficiency?
faq.answer: About 42% of US adults are vitamin D deficient. Risk factors: dark skin, northern latitude, obesity, limited sun exposure, and being over 65.

faq.question: Can I get enough from sun?
faq.answer: 15-20 minutes of midday sun on arms and legs produces about 10,000 IU. However, sunscreen, latitude, season, and skin color affect production. Most people need supplementation.

faq.question: Is the Vitamin D Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Vitamin D Calculator?
sections.body: The Vitamin D Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your current level, weight, target level, you get instant results including current status, recommended daily dose, est. time to target. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting current status right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current level and need to find the right current status. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
